title: grahamia frutescens
intro: Grahamia frutescens, also known as the tree lucerne or Chilean tree lucerne, is a perennial shrub native to South America, particularly in the Andes mountains. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. Its health benefits include treating digestive issues, wounds, and skin conditions, as well as having antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Grahamia frutescens is commonly prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used as a topical application for its medicinal properties.
